findings.description: Each log() reads the entire log file then writes it back with an appended line; this scales poorly and can lose data under concurrency.
findings.title: Logger appends by read+overwrite (O(n) growth and race risk)
findings.suggested_issue: Use append if available or keep bounded logs / separate per-entry files.

findings.description: queue.shift() is O(n); large DAGs could suffer.
findings.title: Topological level computation uses queue.shift() (O(n^2) worst-case)

findings.description: learn:plan passes a large cliPrompt as a single shell argument; may exceed OS limits or be slow.
findings.title: Passing large prompts via -p risks command-length limits
